name: Occitan

ancestors.notes: Gaulish substrate: S. Gaul varieties, less Frankish overlay than N. French; cf. Lambert (2003)
Visigothic superstrate: Toulouse kingdom (418-507 CE), strongest Germanic influence on Occitan
Basque substrate: primarily in Gascon dialect area; h- aspiration, loss of Latin f-, unique definite article eth/era; cf. Rohlfs (1970)
Arabic adstrate: minor, via Iberian transmission

notes: Based on Languedocien/general Occitan norms. Substantial dialectal variation across Gascon, Provençal, Limousin, Auvergnat, and Vivaro-Alpine.
